axis.rationale: Extends slot722 (Brocard n in [2,500]) to [501,1000] via chunked native_decide. Brocard unbounded remains open; this is a publishable research-note range bump.

Realistic Aristotle outcome on E938 via Frey-Hellegouarch + Ribet kernel-control: a sub-claim (bounded-kernel nonexistence or empty-newform-space at explicit small kernels), NOT full finiteness. Per F5: only if Aristotle constructs the Frey curve and applies level-lowering to derive contradiction at the predicted weight-2 cusp form is this a real cross-domain advance; the moving-conductor barrier and fixed exponents (2,3) make full closure 4/10 plausibility. Submit on --rubric-points; tracked as fusion-lane first end-to-end test, not as E938 closure.

Realistic Aristotle outcome on E938 via Frey-Hellegouarch + Ribet kernel-control: a sub-claim (bounded-kernel nonexistence or empty-newform-space at explicit small kernels), NOT full finiteness. Per F5: only if Aristotle constructs the Frey curve and applies level-lowering to derive contradiction at the predicted weight-2 cusp form is this a real cross-domain advance; the moving-conductor barrier and fixed exponents (2,3) make full closure 4/10 plausibility. Submit on --rubric-points; tracked as fusion-lane first end-to-end test, not as E938 closure.

Closure of φ(n)=φ(n+1) infinitude under the two-pillar approach (S-unit/Subspace stratification + Tao 2016 entropy decrement). Full closure is implausible: the Subspace Theorem yields fixed-support finiteness (Pillar 1) but cannot itself reach the unbounded-support contribution; Tao's entropy decrement was a parity-barrier-crossing technique for two-point correlations of completely multiplicative functions and does not directly produce lower bounds for equality of φ at consecutive integers. The realistic sub-claim Aristotle may close is structural: a finite-support reduction theorem that for fixed (r,s) bounds solutions effectively, making this a journal_subclaim. Submit on --rubric-points override; tracked as second end-to-end fusion-lane validation (E938 was first).

axis.rationale: Erdős 324 variant.quintic (a^5+b^5 distinct for a<b) is open in general; this submission targets the S1 bounded native_decide version for a,b ≤ 50. Python scan to N=1000 confirms zero collisions (500500 distinct sums). Lean theorem reduces to a finite decidable check. Unbounded statement remains open — this is a publishable bounded subclaim, not the full conjecture.

Erdős 319 asks for c(N) = max |A| over A ⊆ {1..N} with a sign pattern making Σ δ(n)/n = 0 and no nonempty proper subset of A also vanishing. The full problem is `answer(sorry)` form (S5 F1 trap). This sub-claim sidesteps F1 by stating a concrete explicit-witness lower bound at N=6: A={1,2,3,6} with δ=(−1,+1,+1,+1) gives −1+1/2+1/3+1/6=0 and every nonempty proper subset has nonzero signed sum. Brute-force scan: c(N)=0 for N≤5, c(N)=4 for 6≤N≤11, c(12)=6. Decidability is clean — all conditions are finite-rational equalities/inequalities on a finite Finset; `decide` or `native_decide` discharges the witness. This is a journal_subclaim (bounded-N explicit value), not a full closure of the unbounded c(N) question, which remains genuinely open (Croot/Adenwalla 1−1/e lower bound is the state of the art).

details.ack: AlphaProof Jan 7 2026 partial is on the X^2 generalization (a*X^2+b*X+c, a|b). AlphaProof Nexus ATTEMPTED erdos_477.variants.X_pow_three (in erdos_problems_attempted.txt) but produced NO proof (absent from APNOutputs/ErdosProblems/). teorth wiki lists only variant partial, not X^3. Sekanina (1959) closed only X^2. X^3 case explicitly open in formal-conjectures (theorem erdos_477.X_pow_three, @[category research open]).

axis.rationale: Mechanical extension of L3 iter1's N=50 closure (UUID 229acf74-61e6-4042-9dee-3be9a9815b3e, sorry-free Fin 51 + native_decide) to N=200. Python scan confirms zero collisions for 0 ≤ a < b ≤ 200 (20100 distinct sums; max sum 6.32·10^11). Pure mechanical bound extension via suffices reduction to Fin 201 + native_decide. Unbounded statement remains open; this is a stronger bounded subclaim, requires --rubric-points.

notes: Submitted with Grok-4 synthesis (fit=0.25). Codex/gpt-5.5 finished AFTER submission with rigorous fit=0.07 critique citing: (1) Maier 1985 misattribution (it's Michigan Math J 32(2), 221-225; Adv Math 1981 is the chains-of-gaps paper); (2) Selberg sieve gives upper bounds only, not lower density; (3) van Doorn arXiv:2605.06697 constructs infinitely many powerful 3-term APs at d=2sqrt(N)+1, evidence the conjecture itself may be false; (4) powerful numbers not controlled by coprimality to primorial Q, so Maier matrix method doesn't transplant. Gemini 2.5 Pro and Flash quota-exhausted (429 from Google Cloud).

named_technique: Bourgain-Glibichuk multiplicative energy + Mollin-Walsh consecutive-square obstruction on Golomb powerful set
notes: Wave-3 fresh angle. Critical: wave-3 prompt cited van Doorn 2025 arXiv:2605.06697 which DOES NOT EXIST (Grok confirmed: arxiv id 2605 = May 2026 future-dated, malformed). Replaced with real published anchors: Bourgain-Glibichuk IMRN 2009 doi:10.1093/imrn/rnn104 with c=1/9-o(1); Mollin-Walsh JNT 1986 21:231-243 consecutive-triple obstruction; Heath-Brown Math Comp 1988 50:155-168 consecutive-pair infinitude; Chan 2022 arXiv:2210.00281 abc-conditional. Submission via background monitor (90s retry loop); UUID/slot will be appended once successful.
verified_e938_statement: Let A={n_1<n_2<...} be the sequence of powerful numbers (p|n => p^2|n). Are there only finitely many three-term progressions of consecutive terms n_k, n_{k+1}, n_{k+2}? (formal-conjectures repo ErdosProblems/938.lean confirmed)

details.ack: Wiki labels 'partial AI progress' = AlphaProof Nexus attempted erdos_477.variants.X_pow_three but produced no proof; listed in erdos_problems_attempted.txt but absent from APNOutputs/ErdosProblems/. The X^3 case remains open per the formal-conjectures theorem erdos_477.X_pow_three sorry status (verified 2026-05-31). arXiv:2512.15407 (Cilleruelo r-th powers, 2026) is strictly weaker (exact-on-average not unique-representation). W4-D dossier in research_fusion/runs/erdos_477/.

Wiki 1(a) entry refers to Pratt 2024 and Tao-Teravainen 2025 results on the SISTER problem E257 (omega(n)/2^n), not E251. The E251 problem (n-th prime divided by 2^n) remains OPEN. Prior Aristotle attempts (slots 699/704/1219) returned compiled_no_advance using 2-adic methods. This submission uses a fundamentally different angle: Adamczewski-Bugeaud digit complexity + prime-binary diversity from Guth-Maynard 2024.
